content: 3.5 chars-per-token ratio for token estimation. Reason: Conservative ratio that overestimates slightly (safer — avoids overflow on models with aggressive tokenizers). Real-world English text averages ~4 chars/token, code ~3 chars/token. 3.5 is a safe middle ground.

Keyword-based task classification, no LLM. Reason: Using an LLM to classify tasks for cost routing defeats the purpose (adds cost). Keyword matching is zero-cost, zero-latency, and deterministic. Complex tasks default to cloud (safety-first).

60-second TTL for health check cache. Reason: Balance between responsiveness (detect server restart quickly) and avoiding per-turn latency. 60s means at most 1 health check per minute, and model servers typically stay up/down for minutes, not seconds.
